news.title:
GMDC led gains with a 20% jump; Adani Power, Sonata Software, YES Bank also advanced.
Supreme Industries and Tejas Networks topped losers, down 4.5% and 3.7%, respectively.

news.title:
GMDC sees momentum; partial profit booking advised, re-entry near Rs 660 suggested.
Hold Marico amid Q4 concerns; avoid LIC due to weak valuation below IPO price.

news.title:
GMDC shares surged 18.2% to a 52-week high of ₹736, driven by metal sector rally.
Hindalco, Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc, and others gained 2-3%, boosting overall sentiment.
news.summary:
GMDC shares surged 18.2% to a 52-week high of ₹736, driven by metal sector rally.
Hindalco, Vedanta, Hindustan Zinc, and others gained 2-3%, boosting overall sentiment.

news.title:
Kush Bohra recommends MCX, GMDC, and IMFA for short-term gains; MCX targets Rs 2,815-2,885 with Rs 2,700 stop loss.
GMDC targets Rs 633-650, stop loss Rs 595; IMFA targets Rs 1,418-1,450, stop loss Rs 1,355, based on technical signals.

news.title:
SEBI warned Gujarat Mineral Development Corp for incomplete environmental clearance disclosure.
No monetary penalty imposed; company advised to enhance compliance to avoid future issues.
news.summary:
SEBI warned Gujarat Mineral Development Corp for incomplete environmental clearance disclosure.
No monetary penalty imposed; company advised to enhance compliance to avoid future issues.

news.title:
Tata Motors to raise commercial vehicle prices by 1.5% from April 1, 2026; Sun Pharma's sBLA for ILUMYA accepted by US FDA.
RVNL wins ₹95.3 crore contract in Chhattisgarh; GMDC signs MoU with NMDC for rare earth collaboration in Gujarat.
news.summary:
Tata Motors to raise commercial vehicle prices by 1.5% from April 1, 2026; Sun Pharma's sBLA for ILUMYA accepted by US FDA.
RVNL wins ₹95.3 crore contract in Chhattisgarh; GMDC signs MoU with NMDC for rare earth collaboration in Gujarat.

news.title: GMDC partners with NMDC to explore rare earth elements sector opportunities.
news.summary: GMDC partners with NMDC to explore rare earth elements sector opportunities.

news.title:
GMDC shares in focus after MoU with NMDC to explore rare earth opportunities, including mining, processing, and downstream applications.
GMDC stock rose 116% YoY but fell 6% in a month; 2026 YTD decline is 11%, with a 52-week high of ₹651 and low of ₹245.
news.summary:
GMDC shares in focus after MoU with NMDC to explore rare earth opportunities, including mining, processing, and downstream applications.
GMDC stock rose 116% YoY but fell 6% in a month; 2026 YTD decline is 11%, with a 52-week high of ₹651 and low of ₹245.

news.title:
GMDC signed MoU with NTPC to explore coal supply from Odisha's Baitarni West mine.
Collaboration aims to ensure energy security and affordable power for NTPC projects.
news.summary:
GMDC signed MoU with NTPC to explore coal supply from Odisha's Baitarni West mine.
Collaboration aims to ensure energy security and affordable power for NTPC projects.

news.title:
GMDC gains clearance for Lakhpat Punrajpur mine with 3 MTPA lignite, 29.8 MTPA limestone.
Approval strengthens production visibility, diversifies minerals, and supports energy, cement sectors.
news.summary:
GMDC gains clearance for Lakhpat Punrajpur mine with 3 MTPA lignite, 29.8 MTPA limestone.
Approval strengthens production visibility, diversifies minerals, and supports energy, cement sectors.

news.title:
Jio Financial infused Rs 2,000 crore into Jio Credit for growth; HCLTech signed an MoU with IIT Kanpur for GCC solutions.
Hindalco's Aluchem deal faces US regulatory delay; NTPC, GMDC signed MoUs for coal and lignite resource utilization.
news.summary:
Jio Financial infused Rs 2,000 crore into Jio Credit for growth; HCLTech signed an MoU with IIT Kanpur for GCC solutions.
Hindalco's Aluchem deal faces US regulatory delay; NTPC, GMDC signed MoUs for coal and lignite resource utilization.

news.title:
GMDC and NTPC sign MoU to explore coal, lignite gasification.
Focus on syngas commercialisation, pilot projects, and feasibility studies.
news.summary:
GMDC and NTPC sign MoU to explore coal, lignite gasification.
Focus on syngas commercialisation, pilot projects, and feasibility studies.

news.title:
Gujarat Mineral's Q3 EBITDA rose to 1.01B INR from 930M INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 17.4% from 14.2% YoY.
news.summary:
Gujarat Mineral's Q3 EBITDA rose to 1.01B INR from 930M INR YoY.
EBITDA margin improved to 17.4% from 14.2% YoY.

news.title:
GMDC's Q3 net profit drops 10% YoY to ₹133 crore; revenue falls 11.3% to ₹579 crore.
EBITDA improves 9.5% YoY to ₹101.2 crore, with margin up to 17.5% from 14.1%.
news.summary:
GMDC's Q3 net profit drops 10% YoY to ₹133 crore; revenue falls 11.3% to ₹579 crore.
EBITDA improves 9.5% YoY to ₹101.2 crore, with margin up to 17.5% from 14.1%.

news.title:
Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation (GMDC) released unaudited standalone and consolidated financial results for Q3FY26 and nine months ending Dec 31, 2025.
Total standalone income reached ₹579.1 crore in Q3. Exceptional GST input credit of ₹474.4 crore was recognized due to tax rate changes.
news.summary:
Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation (GMDC) released unaudited standalone and consolidated financial results for Q3FY26 and nine months ending Dec 31, 2025.
Total standalone income reached ₹579.1 crore in Q3. Exceptional GST input credit of ₹474.4 crore was recognized due to tax rate changes.

news.title:
Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation Ltd's board to meet on 13 Feb 2026.
Meeting to approve unaudited financial results for Q3 FY2025-26, both standalone and consolidated.
news.summary:
Gujarat Mineral Development Corporation Ltd's board to meet on 13 Feb 2026.
Meeting to approve unaudited financial results for Q3 FY2025-26, both standalone and consolidated.

news.title:
GMDC shares rose 3% and NMDC recovered 0.4% after FM announced rare-earth corridors in Budget 2026.
Nalco and Hindustan Zinc shares fell 7.9% and 8.6% respectively, amid a broader metals sell-off.
news.summary:
GMDC shares rose 3% and NMDC recovered 0.4% after FM announced rare-earth corridors in Budget 2026.
Nalco and Hindustan Zinc shares fell 7.9% and 8.6% respectively, amid a broader metals sell-off.
